metadata.title: ggen Definition of Done — 5 Core Quality Gates
metadata.description: Authoritative specification for work completion. ALL five gates must pass before code is considered complete. This is non-negotiable.
metadata.entry_point: All gates invoked via `just` entry point (Cargo recipes in justfile)

golden_rule.title: Definition of Done
golden_rule.requirement: ALL FIVE gates must pass. A partial pass is a failure.
golden_rule.violation_severity: CRITICAL — blocks any merge, any deployment, any claim of completion

gates.description: Verify that the `timeout` command exists in the system. Required for all timed gates. This is a safety check preventing silent timeout failures.

success_criteria.description: Command exits cleanly and prints success message

root_cause_diagnosis: Run `command -v timeout` to verify presence
If absent, install: `brew install coreutils` (macOS) or `sudo apt install coreutils` (Linux)
On Windows (WSL), ensure WSL tools are in PATH
recovery_steps: Install timeout tool for your platform

andon_signal.message: STOP. Cannot proceed without timeout command. Install prerequisite.

notes: This gate must pass BEFORE any other timed gate (check, test, lint, slo-check)
It is a prerequisite check, not a compilation/test gate
Failure here indicates environment misconfiguration, not code issues

gates.description: Compilation check across all 15 workspace crates. Verifies that the code compiles without errors. This gate runs fastest for rapid developer feedback.

success_criteria.compilation_status: All workspace crates compile without errors
success_criteria.description: Cargo check completes without ERROR messages

root_cause_diagnosis: Read the compiler error output carefully (error[E...] codes)
Note the crate name and line number from the error
Identify the root cause: type mismatch, missing dependency, lifetime issue, etc.
Do NOT suppress with #[allow(...)] — understand the error first
recovery_steps: Open the source file at the reported line number
Read the full error message and suggestion
Apply the fix directly (not a workaround)
common_causes: Type mismatch: function expects T, but received U
Missing dependency: crate not added to Cargo.toml
Lifetime issue: borrowed value does not live long enough
Trait bound not satisfied: type does not implement required trait
Workspace-wide propagation: change in one crate breaks dependents

andon_signal.message: STOP THE LINE. Compiler errors block all further work.
andon_signal.action: Fix immediately. Do not proceed to lint/test/slo-check. Do not commit.

gates.verification_command: just check && echo '✅ Gate passed: compilation clean'
notes: This is the fastest feedback loop for developers
Runs across ALL targets (binary, tests, examples, docs)
Failure here indicates code that will not compile under CI
Build artifacts are cached, so subsequent checks are 1-5s
The workspace includes 15 crates: verify `cargo build --workspace` matches `just check` output

gates.description: Full test suite — unit tests, integration tests, and property-based tests. Verifies correctness across all test categories. This gate proves the code works as intended.
gates.actual_command_executed: timeout 30s cargo test --workspace --tests (or 120s if first build)

success_criteria.description: No FAILED or panicked tests. All assertions pass.

root_cause_diagnosis: Read the FAILED test output carefully
Note the test name and assertion that failed
Understand: is this a test bug or a code bug?
Read the assertion message and the actual vs expected values
Trace backwards: which code path led to this failure?
recovery_steps: Read the failing test code in crates/*/tests/
Understand what the test is verifying
Apply the fix to the code (not the test)
common_causes: Off-by-one error in loop or slice indexing
State not initialized correctly in test setup
Async function not awaited properly
Error handling code path not implemented
Thread safety issue exposed under concurrent load
Floating-point precision issue in numerical computation
forbidden_responses: Do NOT use #[should_panic] to hide failures
Do NOT mock the failing code — test real behavior

andon_signal.message: STOP THE LINE. Failing tests mean the code is broken.
andon_signal.action: Fix the code. Do not proceed. Do not commit failing tests.

chicago_tdd_requirement.rule: Chicago TDD ONLY — no mocks, no test doubles
chicago_tdd_requirement.explanation: Tests must verify real behavior against real collaborators (databases, filesystems, APIs)

gates.verification_command: just test && echo '✅ Gate passed: all tests pass'
notes: This is the primary correctness gate for the entire project
Tests include: unit (lib.rs tests), integration (tests/ directory), property (proptest), doc examples
First build may take up to 120s if dependencies need compilation
Subsequent runs with hot cache are typically <30s
The justfile has escalation logic: if test >30s, automatically escalates timeout to 120s
Test count: 347+ tests across ggen-core, ggen-cli-lib, ggen-config, ggen-marketplace, ggen-graph, ggen-lsp, genesis-*
Coverage requirement: 80%+ of public APIs tested
No test output suppression — read full logs if failure occurs

gates.description: Clippy linter with -D warnings enforcement + rustfmt code formatting check. Verifies code quality, style consistency, and catches real bugs that the compiler misses.

sub_gates.name: fmt-check
sub_gates.description: Rustfmt code formatting validation (no modifications, dry-run)

sub_gates.name: clippy
sub_gates.description: Clippy lint analysis with -D warnings enforced

success_criteria.formatting: All files formatted per rustfmt rules
success_criteria.clippy: Zero clippy warnings (treated as errors with -D)
success_criteria.description: Code is formatted consistently and contains no linting violations

root_cause_diagnosis: Separate clippy violations from formatting issues
For clippy: Read the warning message and explanation
For formatting: Run `cargo fmt` to auto-fix (then re-check)
Do NOT use `#[allow(clippy::...)]` to suppress — understand the warning first
recovery_steps_formatting: Review changes with `git diff` to ensure readability
Re-run `just lint` to verify formatting check passes
recovery_steps_clippy: Read the clippy warning message carefully
Understand the suggestion (often includes code example)
Apply the fix (refactor the code to satisfy the lint rule)
common_causes: Unused variable or import: clippy detects dead code
Manual type conversion: use `.into()` or `.from()` instead
Unnecessary clone: remove .clone() when not needed
Formatting mismatch: use `cargo fmt` to auto-fix
Inefficient pattern: clippy suggests more idiomatic Rust
Match arm simplification: collapse duplicate arms
forbidden_responses: Do NOT add `#[allow(...)]` to suppress warnings
Do NOT leave `#[allow(...)]` from previous debugging
Do NOT use `//! allow-warnings` in module headers
Do NOT commit code with clippy warnings

andon_signal.message: STOP before release. Warnings are not acceptable in production code.
andon_signal.action: Fix all clippy warnings. Do not commit with -D warnings violations.

gates.verification_command: just lint && echo '✅ Gate passed: code clean and formatted'
notes: Clippy enforces -D warnings (warnings treated as errors) — zero tolerance
Rustfmt enforces consistent indentation, line length (100 chars), and spacing
This gate catches real bugs: unused variables, inefficient patterns, etc.
Clippy often provides explicit suggestions — follow them
Do NOT use #[allow(...)] as a reflex — understand the warning first
Format check runs first (fast), clippy analysis second (slower)
All targets checked: binary, lib, tests, examples, benches

gates.description: Performance SLO (Service Level Objective) validation. Verifies that build times, runtime, and memory usage stay within acceptable bounds. Prevents performance regressions.

success_criteria.performance_metrics: All SLOs met within defined thresholds
success_criteria.description: Build times and runtime performance stay within SLO targets

root_cause_diagnosis: Identify which SLO target was exceeded
Measure current performance: `cargo bench --bench cli_startup_performance`
Compare to SLO threshold
Identify what changed: new dependencies? expensive algorithm? allocation?
Profile the bottleneck using flamegraph or perf
recovery_steps: Locate the hot code path (where time is spent)
Apply optimization: reduce allocations, cache results, parallelize, etc.
Re-run `just slo-check` to verify SLO met
common_causes: New dependency added: Cargo.lock or Cargo.toml causing rebuild
Algorithm complexity: O(n²) loop instead of O(n) or O(n log n)
Memory allocation in loop: move allocation outside loop
Unnecessary cloning: remove .clone() calls
Blocking I/O in async context: use async operations
Redundant recompilation: dep changed affecting workspace

andon_signal.message: STOP before release. Performance regressions block deployment.
andon_signal.action: Fix performance bottleneck. Do not ship with SLO violations.

gates.verification_command: just slo-check && echo '✅ Gate passed: performance SLOs met'
notes: SLO targets are defined in performance.md and Cargo.toml [bench] sections
Benchmarks are measured on the current hardware (results vary by machine)
This gate validates operational budget: do not exceed defined limits
Determinism is verified by running same input multiple times and checking output hash
First build SLO <15s ensures developer feedback loop remains fast
Incremental build SLO <2s ensures edit-compile-test cycle is responsive
RDF processing SLO <5s/1k triples ensures sync remains under 10s for typical projects

pre_commit_gate.description: Combined gate that runs all preceding checks in sequence: fmt-check → check → lint → test-lib. Used for pre-commit hook and local validation.

success_criteria.description: All four sub-gates pass without failure

pre_commit_gate.failure_behavior: Stops at first failure; does not proceed to subsequent gates
use_cases: Pre-commit hook (blocks commit if gate fails)
CI gating (blocks merge if gate fails)

complete_gate_sequence.description: Full quality assurance pipeline for release-ready code
complete_gate_sequence.command_sequence: just timeout-check && just check && just lint && just test && just slo-check
complete_gate_sequence.definition_of_done_met_when: All five gates pass without intervention
complete_gate_sequence.blockers: Any single gate failure blocks release and all subsequent steps

andon_protocol.description: Stop-the-line discipline when quality gates fail

signal_levels.action: HALT immediately. Do not proceed. Do not commit.
signal_levels.message: Compiler errors or failing tests = code is broken

signal_levels.action: STOP before release. Fix before shipping.
signal_levels.message: Code quality or performance violations cannot be merged

required_behavior: 2. Stop all other work (not after next function, not after next file — NOW)
8. Only then proceed to next gate

evidence_and_proof.requirement: Every claim of completion must include proof

proof_formats.proof: Exit code 0 from `cargo test --workspace --tests` with test count and pass rate
Exit code 0 from clippy and rustfmt with zero warnings
Benchmark output showing all metrics within SLO thresholds

otel_validation.applies_to: Any feature involving LLM calls, external APIs, or pipeline stages
otel_validation.requirement: OTEL spans must exist with correct attributes

no_fabrication_rule: Never claim a feature works without running tests
Never show mock output as proof of external calls
Never fabricate OTEL spans or traces
Real evidence (code + test output + OTEL) only

pre_commit_hook.note: Even doc-only commits must pass all gates (entire workspace compiles)

stages: test (120s timeout, escalates on first build)
ci_pipeline.failure_blocks: PR cannot be merged until all gates pass

workspace_context.testing_framework: Chicago TDD ONLY (no mocks, real collaborators)

quick_reference.all_five_gates: just timeout-check && just check && just lint && just test && just slo-check
quick_reference.pre_commit: just pre-commit (fastest local validation: fmt-check + check + lint + test-lib)
quick_reference.stop_the_line_protocol: Any gate failure = HALT, fix, re-run until pass
quick_reference.no_partial_passes: All five gates must pass. One failure = not done.
